State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444


January 21, 1983


Helen E. Jackson
San Jose City Clerk
601 N. First Street
San Jose, CA 95110

Re: A-83-013

Dear Ms. Jackson:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to Janis Shank McLean, an
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political
Practices Commission.  If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at
(916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        [illegible] A. Shulman
                                        Chief
                                        Legal Division

BAM:km
Enclosure

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · Administration · · Executive/Legal · · Enforcement · · Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901          322-6441              322-6444

February 9, 1983


Ms. Helen E. Jackson
City Clerk
801 North First Street
San Jose, CA 95110
                                          Re: Our Advice No. A-83-013

Dear Ms. Jackson:

     Thank you for your letter concerning the Socialist Workers
Campaign Committee. You stated that the Committee filed a
consolidated campaign statement (Form 490) disclosing one
contribution for $100, but failing to disclose its date of
receipt, or the name, address, occupation, or employer of the
contributor. The basis for this nondisclosure is asserted to be
the U.S. Supreme Court's holdings in Buckley v. Valeo and Brown,
et al., v. Socialist Workers '74 Campaign Committee (Ohio).

     The Committee's nondisclosure constitutes an apparent
violation of the Political Reform Act. You should refer the
matter to the Santa Clara County District Attorney's Office
(Government Code Section 81010(d)) who will determine how it
wishes to proceed.

     Thank you for your letter. If you have any questions,
please feel free to contact me at 916/322-5901.

                                          Very truly yours,

                                          [signature]
                                          Janis Shank McLean
                                          Counsel
                                          Legal Division

JSM:km

CITY OF SAN JOSÉ, CALIFORNIA

801 NORTH FIRST STREET
SAN JOSE, CA 95110
(408) 277-4424

CITY CLERK

January 17, 1983

Tom Houston, Executive Director
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
100 K Street Building
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Houston:

Pursuant to Section 33114 of the Government Code, the City of
San Jose requests an opinion regarding the following:

(1) On July 30, 1982, the Socialist Workers Campaign Committee,
a controlled committee supporting Tom Tomasko for Mayor of
the City of San Jose, California, filed a consolidated
campaign statement (Form 490) disclosing one contribution
of $100 but failing to disclose its date of receipt, name,
address, occupation and employer of the contributor;

(2) The Socialist Workers Campaign Committee (SWCC) and Tom
Tomasko assert that the SWCC is a minor party subject to
harassment; and that pursuant to Buckley v. Valeo and
Brown, et al, v. Socialist Workers '74 Campaign Committee
(Ohio), they are not required to disclose the identity of
contributors.

Therefore, please advise me of my responsibility pursuant to
Government Code Section 81010(d). Is the failure of the candidate
and the SWCC to disclose the identity of the contributor and
"apparent violation" of the Fair Political Practices Act requiring
the City Clerk to report same to the District Attorney?

It may be appropriate for the Commission to consider an
emergency regulation providing direction for local agencies under
these circumstances.

Sincerely,

HELEN E. JACKSON
City Clerk
